channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Here’s how Google describes its fee-reducing Apps Experience and Games Level Up programs
item.description: Today, Google killed its 30 percent app store fee, partially uncoupled Google Play from Google Play Billing after they were declared an illegal monopoly in the US, and much more. From July, depending on where you live, Google will now generally charge developers 20 percent for in-app purchases, or 10 percent for subscriptions - but […]

item.title: The $100 Billion OpenAI-Nvidia Deal Is Not Happening
item.description: The deal that once sparked circular dealmaking fears is no more, according to Jensen Huang.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg downplays Meta's own research in New Mexico child safety trial
item.description: Jurors in a New Mexico child safety trial heard testimony from Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg today. During pre-recorded testimony, Zuckerberg was repeatedly asked about the company's understanding of social media addiction and other issues that had been studied by its researchers. During the deposition, which was recorded last March, Zuckerberg was asked about numerous findings from researchers at Meta who studied how the company's apps affect users and teens. The CEO downplayed the significance of many of these documents.Early in the testimony, which was viewed by Engadget on Courtroom View Networ...

item.title: Big Tech Signs White House Data Center Pledge With Good Optics and Little Substance
item.description: “Data centers … they need some PR help,”President Donald Trump said at the event.

item.title: Bill Gates-backed TerraPower begins nuclear reactor construction
item.description: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission has granted approval to TerraPower to begin construction of a reactor in Wyoming. The project is the first new US commercial nuclear reactor in about a decade, according to The New York Times. TerraPower was founded by Bill Gates, and it took years for the business to receive regulatory approval for this construction effort. TerraPower is part of a push to create more efficient and less expensive nuclear facilities as an alternative power source, particularly as AI companies and data center construction places more demands on the US' current infrastructure. Te...

item.title: RFK Jr. Is Now Taking Aim at Dunkin’ Donuts
item.description: The health secretary recently announced his plans to have companies like Dunkin' Donuts prove their foods are safe to eat.

item.title: ‘Project Hail Mary’ Used a Surprising Amount of Practical Effects
item.description: Directors Phil Lord and Christopher Miller wanted to balance CGI effects with real sets and physical elements as much as possible.

item.title: What AI Models for War Actually Look Like
item.description: While companies like Anthropic debate limits on military uses of AI, Smack Technologies is training models to plan battlefield operations.

item.title: If Alien Dyson Spheres Are Real, These Are the Stars They’d Pick
item.description: Assuming intelligent aliens know how to harvest energy from stars, would humanity be able to spot these high-level structures?

item.title: Epic and Google have signed a special deal for a new class of ‘metaverse’ apps
item.description: Epic Games and Google are burying the hatchet, but documents released today reveal that they aren't only aligned on how Google is shaking things up for app stores. The two companies have also agreed to terms about a new class of apps that they're calling "metaverse browsers," according to a heavily redacted section of a […]

item.title: 'We wanted something that felt fun and friendly, and fresh, and felt like it really suited the spirit of this product': Apple exec on why their new budget laptop is called MacBook Neo
item.description: Don't blame The Matrix

item.title: Apple Music to add Transparency Tags to distinguish AI music, says report
item.description: The label or distributor has to opt in to tagging their music as AI, so it's unclear how effective this intervention will be.

item.title: I've hand-picked the 15 best iPhone 17e cases to keep your new phone safe, while still looking cool
item.description: Protective and stylish cases, direct from Apple and beyond.

item.title: 'We can accelerate the adoption of post-quantum resilience for all web users': Google reveals how Chrome will help secure HTTPS certificates against quantum computer attacks — without breaking the Internet
item.description: Google plans to defend HTTPS certificates against quantum attacks using compact verification methods that preserve browser performance and system compatibility.

item.title: Magico's new S7 money-no-object speakers were acoustically refined using robotics — and I'm desperate to hear them
item.description: Magico's exhaustive pursuit of audio excellence harnesses cutting edge tech — and understandably, that doesn't come cheap

item.title: Your brand is only as strong as the leaders who carry it
item.description: A brand doesn’t live in positioning statements or buzzy marketing statements. At the Exceptional Women Alliance, we enable high-level women to mentor each other to achieve personal and professional happiness through sisterhood. As the nonprofit organization’s founder, chair, and CEO, I am honored to interview and share insights from thought leaders who are part of our peer-to-peer mentoring. ...

item.title: Can an AI chatbot be held responsible for a user’s death? A lawsuit against Google’s Gemini is about to test that
item.description: The father of Jonathan Gavalas alleges Gemini fueled delusions, sent him on violent “missions,” and ultimately encouraged self-harm. Google says its AI is designed to prevent such outcomes. A Florida man initially began using Google’s Gemini AI platform last August for assistance with typical queries. By early October, the chatbot had driven him to commit suicide, claims a lawsuit filed against the tech giant on Wednesday....

item.title: Assassin's Creed Unity is getting a free 60 fps patch tomorrow
item.description: Ubisoft shared its upcoming plans for the Assassin's Creed franchise today. Along with the news of a remake for its piratical entry, the game company also announced that a visual upgrade is coming for a title from way back in 2014. Assassin's Creed Unity will receive a free patch tomorrow to offer 60 fps performance on the PlayStation 5 and the Xbox Series X/S. The company bringing a performance upgrade more than a decade after launch feels like a fitting close to Unity's development. The game suffered from bugs and performance issues from the jump, and while most of those did get addressed, n...

item.title: The Plague That Changed the Course of ‘Game of Thrones’ History
item.description: Just a year after the events of 'A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms' season one, Westeros' course was changed not by a war but by a terrible sickness.

item.title: LG reveals pricing for its 2026 OLED TVs
item.description: Now for the news you've been waiting with bated breath for: LG's 2026 TVs from CES finally have prices. (Well, some of them do, anyway.) Surprisingly, the evo G6 and C6 series OLED TVs aren't increasing in cost from last year's models. But the bad news is, they’re still expensive as all get-out.The flagship LG evo G6 series ranges in price from $2,499 to $24,999. (Cue spit take.) Fortunately, that five-figure price only applies to the 97-inch model, which nobody this side of Elon Musk needs. The entry-level price is for a 55-inch OLED. Moving up the ladder, the 65-inch one costs $3,399, the 77...
